coredata.dc:title: Structural, physico-chemical landscapes, ground state and excited state properties in different solvent atmosphere of Avapritinib and its ultrasensitive detection using SERS/GERS on self-assembly formation with graphene quantum dots

Pooventhiran, T.
Al-Zaqri, Nabil
Rao, D. Jagadeeswara
Thomas, Renjith
coredata.dc:description: Avapritinib is a complex molecule with many heterocyclic rings developed for the treatment of advanced PDGFRA D842V-mutant gastrointestinal stromal tumours. The manuscripts presents the study of the structure, ground state and excited state reactivity and stability indices in different solvent atmosphere using density functional theory in B3LYP/aug-cc-pVDZ level. Different molecular properties like reactivity surface, average local ionization energy, nuclear and electronic electrostatic potential, reduced density gradient assay, and localized molecular orbital analysis are studied in detail. The various intramolecular electron delocalisations were studied using natural orbital formalism and intermolecular interactions analysed by evaluating the extended of non-covalent interactions. Another interesting study is the enhancement in Raman activity of the compound when it forms a self-assembly with graphene quantum dots leading to SERS/GERS, which is studied by incorporating Grimme's dispersion into the previous functional. The electronic spectrum was studied in detail using time dependent density functional theory using long range corrected CAM-B3LYP functionals. The compound showed good light harvesting efficiency also. The local information entropy study provides uncertainty of electrons in spatial distribution.

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Avapritinib is a complex molecule with many heterocyclic rings developed for the treatment of advanced PDGFRA D842V-mutant gastrointestinal stromal tumours. The manuscripts presents the study of the structure, ground state and excited state reactivity and stability indices in different solvent atmosphere using density functional theory in B3LYP/aug-cc-pVDZ level. Different molecular properties like reactivity surface, average local ionization energy, nuclear and electronic electrostatic potential, reduced density gradient assay, and localized molecular orbital analysis are studied in detail. The various intramolecular electron delocalisations were studied using natural orbital formalism and intermolecular interactions analysed by evaluating the extended of non-covalent interactions. Another interesting study is the enhancement in Raman activity of the compound when it forms a self-assembly with graphene quantum dots leading to SERS/GERS, which is studied by incorporating Grimme's dispersion into the previous functional. The electronic spectrum was studied in detail using time dependent density functional theory using long range corrected CAM-B3LYP functionals. The compound showed good light harvesting efficiency also. The local information entropy study provides uncertainty of electrons in spatial distribution.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Introduction
ce:section.ce:para: Avapritinib is a complex molecule developed for the management of patients with advanced PDGFRA D842V-mutant gastrointestinal stromal tumours. It acts as a novel tyrosine kinase inhibitor and interferes with the cancer cell metabolism [1–3]. The IUPAC name of the compound is (1S)-1-(4-fluorophenyl)-1-[2-[4-[6-(1-methylpyrazol-4-yl)pyrrolo[2,1-f][1,2,4]triazin-4-yl]piperazin-1-yl]pyrimidin-5-yl]ethanamine. The compound contains several heterocyclic rings like fluorophenyl, methylpyrazolyl, pyrrolo, triazinyl, piperazinyl and pyrimidinyl ring. Compounds with rings like flourophenyl are already reported to show immense biological activity. Malik et al. has reported the synthesis of biological active piperazinium chlorides with fluorophenyl side chains [4]. Also several Schiff and Mannich bases with 2,4-dichloro-5-fluorophenyl groups are reported to show excellent antimicrobial activities [5]. R. Thomas reported the experimental and computational studies of several pyrazole analogues [6–8] showing a variety of medicinal activities. Several antimycobacterial, anti-inflammatory, antimalarial, anticancer, antipsychotic, antivirals and anticonvulsant activity are shown by organic heterocyclic compounds with pyrole moiety [9–12]. Another hetrocyclic analogue triazene is commonly used as a scaffold for the synthesis of many useful biologically active molecules [13]. Methyltriazine is found to show effective anti-cancer features [14] while derivatives of 1-acyl-6-indolyl-3-phenyl-1,6-dihydro-1,2,4-triazin-5(4H)-ones shows excellent antiviral properties [15]. Piperazine derivatives are useful in the synthesis of novel drug molecules which act as central nervous system modifiers like antipsychotics, antidepressants and anxiolytic action. Benzylpiperazine is an addictive narcotic drug for getting euphoric stimulations [16–18]. Thus all heterocyclic rings in the avapritinib are medicinally active and hence, that might be contributed to the enhanced anticancer property of the compound.

Being a very important novel organic drug molecule, the structural and electronic property based information like stability, reactivity, regioselectivity etc. are very important to study the exact mechanism of action of the molecule, analysis of side effects, design of new useful therapeutic agents from this molecule etc. [19–23]. Detailed study of literature indicated that no much information are available regarding the above. Computational tools can be effectively used to predict the structure, reactivity and stability of the compounds [24–26]. This manuscript is in this direction. We study the ground state behaviour of the compound followed by the excited state properties in different solvent medium, electronic spectra and detailed descriptors based on frontier molecular orbitals, surface topology and charge topology. Also, we evaluated the extend of non-covalent interactions in the molecule, stabilisation using intramolecule electron shifts, average and local ionization enthalpies and also local information entropy of the molecule [27].

Along with the study of synthesis and biological activity, analytical detection of a compound is also very important in pharmaceutical, medicinal and forensic point of view. One of the best method to identify a compound is using Surface Enhanced Raman Spectra (SERS) of a compound. When graphene is used as the surface, it is generally called as Graphene Enhanced Raman Spectra, in which the compound when form a self-assembly with a host molecule shows enhanced Raman activity, which can be used as an analytical tool in the identification of the compound [28,29]. In this manuscript, we attempt to study the formation of the self-assembly of the drug with a graphene quantum dot and the enhancement of the Raman activity of the compound leading to SERS/GERS [30–33].

ce:section.ce:section-title: Methods
ce:section.ce:para: Avapritinib was optimised using Gaussian-09 [34] software and package with DFT- B3LYP with aug- cc-pVDZ basis set [35–37]. We performed the frequency calculations to ensure that there exists no imaginary frequency such that the obtained geometry corresponds to a global minimum for reaching the optimised geometry. Default convergence criteria for SCF and optimization procedures were used in this study. Energy and reactivity descriptors were determined in the ground state and excited state in different solvents. SERS/GERS properties are analysed using B3LYP functional by accommodating Grimme's dispersion correction [38–40]. TD-DFT [41–43]was used to do the excited state studies using B3LYP functional. We used the same geometry for calculating frontier molecular analysis, natural bonding orbitals, and nonlinear optical studies. For UV–visible spectrum simulation, we used TD-DFT with long-range corrected CAM-B3LYP functional [44] with aug-cc-pVDZ as a basis set. Wavefunction properties are calculated using Multiwavefunction [45] software for calculating total electrostatic potential average localized ionization energy and noncovalent interactions [46].

ce:section.ce:section-title: Results and discussions
Geometry and structure of Avapritinib molecule
ce:section.ce:para: Avapritinib is a complex compound with several complex rings. The IUPAC name of the compound is (1S)-1-(4-fluorophenyl)-1-[2-[4-[6-(1-methylpyrazol-4-yl)pyrrolo[2,1-f][1,2,4]triazin-4-yl]piperazin-1-yl]pyrimidin-5-yl]ethanamine. It contains a flourophenyl ring, methylpyrazol ring, pyrole ring, triazine ring, piperazine ring and pyrimidine ring. The structure of the compound is optimised using DFT in the level B3LYP/aug-cc-pVDZ. The optimised structure with atom number is given in the Fig. 1 and the geometry details are given as follows. The bond distances for 1F36C, 2N-12C, 2N-13C, 2N-16C, 3N-14C, 3N-15C, 3N-17C, 4N-9N, 4N-18C, 4N-24C, 5N-16C, 5N-28C, 6N-17C, 6N-25C, 7N-17C, 7N-26C, 8N-19C, 8N-53H, 8N-54H, 9N-28C, 10N-11N, 10N-32C, 10N-37C, 11N-33C, 19C20C, 19C23C, 19C27C, and 22C29C have 1.35, 1.46, 1.46, 1.36, 1.45, 1.46, 1.36, 1.36, 1.40, 1.35, 1.33, 1.35, 1.35, 1.33, 1.35, 1.33, 1.48, 1.02, 1.02, 1.31, 1.34, 1.36, 1.44, 1.33, 1.53, 1.54, 1.52 and 1.45 Å respectively. The bond angles for 12C-2N-13C, 12C-2N-16C, 13C-2N-16C, 14C-3N-15C, 14C-3N-17C, 15C-3N-17C, 9N-4N-18C, 9N-4N-24C, 18C-4N-24C, 16C-5N-28C, 17C-6N-25C, 17C-7N-26C, 19C-8N-53H, 19C-8N-54H, 4N-9N-28C, 11N-10N-32C, 32C-10N-37C, 10N-11N-33C,3N-17C-6N, 3N-17C-7N, 8N-19C-20C, 8N-19C-23C, 8N-19C-27C, 20C-19C-23C, 2N-16C-5N, 2N-16C-18C, 4N-18C-16C, 5N-28C-9N, 4N-24C-22C, 24C-22C-29C, 21C-22C-29C, 22C-29C-32C, 22C-29C-33C, 32C-29C-33C, and 10N-37C-62H are 116.51, 123.27, 120.20, 117.43, 122.23, 120.31, 125.98, 123.86, 110.14, 118.04, 116.14, 115.97, 108.23, 109.37, 112.50, 112.45, 127.81, 104.77, 118.03, 116.22, 108.06, 111.13, 106.06, 110.64, 117.28, 122.76, 114.45, 129.06, 108.44, 125.70, 127.19, 128.30, 128.46, 103.22, and 108.690 respectively.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Ground state and excited state molecular descriptors in different solvent atmosphere
ce:section.ce:para: Frontier molecular orbitals can provide valuable information on the energy band gap and using the HOMO and LUMO energy, one can predict various physical and chemical descriptors of the molecule, which enables us to comment on the reactivity, stability, and biological activity. The energies are calculated in the level B3LYP/aug-cc-pVDZ. The frontier energies and other chemical descriptors are related and the data is presented in Table 1 .

The higher occupied molecular orbital (HOMO) having the energy is −5.39 eV at piperazin-, pyrrolotriazin-, and methylpyrazol- groups, and lower unoccupied molecular orbital (LUMO) having the energy −1.09 eV at fluorophenyl-, 1-ethanamin-, and pyrimidin- groups. The energy gap (ΔE) between conduction band (lower unoccupied molecular orbital) and valence band (higher occupied molecular orbital) having low energy is 4.30 eV and this much energy needs electron transfer from the conduction band to the valence band. The ionization energy (I) is one of the important chemical descriptors, which is always positive value; this much of energy 5.39 eV need avapritinib molecule makes positive ion by eliminated an electron. Avapritinib molecule release 1.09 eV of energy, when adding an electron to make it negative ion, and this much of energy also called its electron affinity (A) value. The energy is 2.15 eV need for all the electrons to stay stable also called global hardness (η) for avapritinib molecule, and the energy is 344.69 eV need for all the electrons became unstable also called global softness (S). The energy 3.24 eV amount require for can absorbs or release a due to a charge of the particle number of the given species in the chemical reaction also called chemical potential (μ), and −3.24 eV amount of energy required for bonded electrons pull towards itself also called electronegativity (χ) property. Avapritinib having the electrophilicity index (ω) value is 2.45 eV much amount of energy need for an increasing amount of electron density by electrophiles additions, and nucleophilicity index (N) value is 302.56 eV much amount of energy need for an increasing amount of nucleus density by the addition of nucleophiles. The properties are different in the excited state.

The excited states are modelled using TD-DFT using B3LYP functional and the same basis set. The gas phase electron descriptors of both GS and ES are almost same but solvents play their own inherent effects in the stabilisation of the excited states. The effect of solvents were analysed using IEFPCM implicit solvation atmosphere available in the Gaussian 09 W. Data shows that ionization energy increases in solvents rather than in the gas phase and electron affinity decreases. The stability can be explained by chemical potential, which shows that the compound is more stable in water and DMSO solvent atmosphere with value of −3.31 eV, where the gas phase is −3.24 eV. The other parameters did not show any change.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Discussion of electronic spectrum and light harvesting energy
ce:section.ce:para: Being a time dependent phenomenon, the electronic transitions and consequently the electronic spectra of compounds cannot be modelled by DFT simulations, instead has to use time-dependent (TD-DFT) simulations which employs Tamm-Dancoff approximations. Numerous studies has been reported using this method [47]. We used TDDFT simulations with CAM-B3LYP functional and aug-cc-pVDZ as a basis set using IEFPCM solvation model [48]with methanol as solvent. The simulated UV spectrum is provided in Fig. S1.

There are two important electronic transitions for avapritinib molecule. The energy, wavelength, oscillator strength and symmetry are 104.59 kcal/mol, 273.35 nm, 0.38 and singlet asymmetry respectively. The major contribution for this transition from second higher occupied molecular orbital (HOMO-2) to lower unoccupied molecular orbital (LUMO) with 11 percentage and, higher occupied molecular orbital (HOMO) to lower unoccupied molecular orbital (LUMO) with 71 percentage, and minor contributions for these transitions are second first higher occupied molecular orbital (HOMO-2) to fourth lower unoccupied molecular orbital (LUMO+4) with 5 percentage, and higher occupied molecular orbital (HOMO) to fourth lower unoccupied molecular orbital (LUMO+4) with 6 percentage. For the second transition the energy, wavelength, oscillator strength and symmetry are 102.77 kcal/mol, 278.18, 0.0818, and singlet asymmetry respectively. The major contributions for this transition are from second higher occupied molecular orbital (HOMO-2) to lower unoccupied molecular orbital (LUMO) with 65 percentage, higher occupied molecular orbital (HOMO) to lower unoccupied molecular orbital (LUMO) with 19 percentage, and higher occupied molecular orbital (HOMO) to fourth lower unoccupied molecular orbital (LUMO+4) with 24 percentage and there is no minor contributions. The frontier molecular orbitals involved in the electronic transitions are given in Fig. 2 . Looking at HOMO and LUMO, they are located at different regions of the molecule, indicating that HOMO to LUMO transition corresponds to intramolecular charge transfer transition.

For the first transition, oscillator strength (f) is 0.3834, which means, the molecule is having good light-harvesting efficiency (LHE) [49–52], which is expressed as a function of the oscillator strength related as LHE = 1–10−f. The value is 0.5863 for the first transition, which means that the compound can absorb 58.63% of the incident light energy for electronic excitation at that particular wavelength. Thus, this compound can be investigated as a compound for harvesting light from UV region in a DSSC [53–56].

ce:section.ce:section-title: Surface enhanced Raman spectral determination of Avapritinib using graphene quantum dots
ce:section.ce:para: It is found that our drug candidate forms self-assemblies with grapheme quantum dots and shows an enhancement in the Raman activity after the formation of self-assembly cluster. To study this the compound, graphene and the self-assembly is optimised with B3LYP functional with Grimme's dispersion correction, to account for the dispersion interaction between the drug and the graphene layer in the self-assembly using the same basis sets as used for the geometry optimisation before. No imaginary frequencies were observed. The absorption energy details are given as follows. The adsorption energy is −19.62 kJ/mol (Table 2).

Thus, the complex between virgin graphene quantum dot and avapritinib is stable. We thought whether this self-assembly formation can be used as a technique to detect the presence of the compound in solution. It is found that there is significant enhancement in Raman activity on adsorption (see Fig. 3 ) using DFT. The significant enhancement is observed at the wavenumber 1592 cm−1, which corresponds to the vibrations of the peripheral CH vibrations of graphene layer on adsorption of the drug. There is 5.99 times enhancement in Raman activity in that region.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Non-linear optical properties of Avapritinib
ce:section.ce:para: Study of light matter interactions is very important especially for organic molecules, as they are rich in delocalisable electrons [57–61]. These properties are very important in deciding their application in various areas of organic electronics. The ability of the molecule to bend the linear light can be done using the polarisability and hyperpolarisability values obtained from the Raman spectrum simulation [62–65]. This type non-linear optical activity is very important for using the compound in organic electronics industry. The simulation is carried out in the same theoretical level as of the optimisation and is compared with a standard non-linear optically active substance urea and p-nitroacetanilide (PNA) [66]. The non-linear optical property data are shown in Table 3 .

Avapritinib having dipole moment (μ) is 5.9257 D, which is 1.9257 times greater than urea and 7.1800 times greater than PNA, hyperpolarizability (β) is 1451.0511 ∗ 10−37 esu, which is 16.4314 times greater than urea and 1.7993 times greater than PNA, mean polarizability (α0) is 374.0359 ∗ 10−23 esu, which is 15.3920 times greater than urea and 3.0444 times greater than PNA, the anisotropy of the polarizability (Δα) is 840.4721 ∗ 10−23 esu, which is 15`8456 times greater than urea and 2.4350 times greater than PNA and molar refractivity (MR) is 9440.3811 esu, which is 15.3949 times greater than urea and 3.0450 times greater than PNA.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Nature bond orbital (NBO) study of Avapritinib
ce:section.ce:para: Intramolecular electron displacements are very important as they decide the inherent stability of a compound. Natural bond orbital analysis is used to study the extend of various electron delocalisation possible in ozanimod [67–69]. This is performed using the NBO suite [70] available along with the Gaussian 09 W suite. The occupancy values of the natural bond orbitals and their deloclaisation energy provides valuable information about the above mentioned stabilisations.

Table S2 explains the summary of natural population charges analysis for avapritinib. Each atom having particular natural charges and population charges are core, valence and Rydberg populations. Avapritinib molecule having the total natural charge is zero, and total natural populations are core is 73.9672, valence is 187.233, and the Rydberg population is 0.80011 and the total population is 262.0000. Table S3 shows the natural populations between natural minimal basis and natural Rydberg basis for avapritinib. Total core population is 73.96724 out of 74 basis, which is more than 99.5 percentage and valence population is 187.23266 out of 188 basis, which is more than 99.5 percentage, the natural minimal basis (NMB) is 261.19989 out of 262 basis, which is more than 99.5 percentage, and natural Rydberg basis (RYB) is 0.80011 out of 262 basis, which is below 0.50 percentage. Table S4 shows the electronic configurations for all the elements in avapritinib. Table S5 explains natural bond analysis by occupancy threshold energy for in avapritinib. For the cycles 1 and 2 having the same occupancy threshold energy 1.9, Lewis occupancy is 248.8051, non-Lewis occupancy is 13.195, and deviation is 0.6. Table S6 shows the total Lewis and non-Lewis contributions for avapritinib. The contributions for Core orbital is 73.96728 out of 74 basis, which is more than 99.5 percentage, Valence Lewis orbital is 180.86860 out of 188 basis, which is 96.2070 percentage and total Lewis contribution is 254.83588 out of 262 basis, which is 97.2660 percentage. The contribution of Valence non-Lewis orbital is 6.59472 out of 262 basis, which is 2.5170 percentage, Rydberg non-Lewis orbital is 0.56940 out of 262 basis, which is 0.2170 percentage and total non-Lewis contribution is 7.16412 out of 262 basis, which is 2.7340 percentage.

Table S7 explains nature bond orbitals (NBOs) for avapritinib by second-order Perturbation theory analysis of Fock matrix in NBO Basis. The transition contributions of donor NBOs numbers are 122, 123, 131, 131, 582, 585, 588, 588, 592, and 602 with donor orbital NBOs are LP(1) N2, LP(1) N3, LP(1) C21, LP(1) C21, BD*(2) N4-C18, BD*(2) N5-C16, BD*(2) N6-C17, BD*(2) N6-C17, BD*(2) N7-C26, and BD*(2) N11-C33 to the acceptor NBOs numbers are 585, 588, 582, 624, 585, 597, 592, 619, 619, and 637 with acceptor orbital NBOs are BD*(2) N5-C16, BD*(2) N6-C17, BD*(2) N4-C18, BD*(2) C22-C24, BD*(2) N5-C16, BD*(2) N9-C28, BD*(2) N7-C26, BD*(2) C20-C25, BD*(2) C20-C25, and BD*(2) C29-C32 by taking the transition energies are 62.08, 60.56, 295.72, 65.82, 156.99, 197.20, 314.56, 87.54, 125.73, and 121.79 kcal/mol respectively.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Study of average localized ionization energy (ALIE)
ce:section.ce:para: The local I(r) average energy of ionization is the energy needed to remove an electron from point r into the system [7,21,71]. The lowest values show the positions of the least tightly-held electrons and therefore the chosen reaction sites with electrophiles or radicals. Beyond its importance to reactive behaviour, I(r) plays an significant role in other fundamental fields, including atomic shell composition, electronegativity and local polarizability and hardness. The pictorial representation of ALIE of avapritinib is given in Fig. 4 .

From the blue in color to red in color as the numerical values between 0.000 and 2.000, and the molecule within ±17.79 Bohr3 range. The color red between the range 1.800 and 2.000 indicates strong localized electrons occur in core electrons in heavy atoms are nitrogens, carbons and fluorine in fluorophenyl-, 1-ethenamin-, pyrimidin-, piperazin-, pyrrolotriazin-, and methylpyrazol- groups. The color blueish-green between the range 0.700 and 0.900, indicates moving electrons or delocalization of electrons in avpritinib molecule at the sites are fluorophenyl-, 1-ethenamin-, pyrimidin-, piperazin-, pyrrolotriazin-, and methylpyrazol-groups, which produce resonance structures, and make the molecule stability. The blue in color range between 0.000 and 0.500 indicates the sigma bond as well as the stable bond between atoms in avapritinib molecule, which the sites are from hydrogens to carbons in the whole molecule, and lone pair of electrons having nitrogens in 1-ethenamin-, pyrimidin-, piperazin-, pyrrolotriazin-, and methylpyrazol- groups.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Electron localized functions (ELF) of Avapritinib
ce:section.ce:para: This study explains further the electronic structure for avapritinib. The higher value of electron localization function is strongly localized and low value is strong delocalization of electron in this molecule [72–74]. The electron localized function (ELF) for avapritinib shown in Fig. 5 .

From the blue in color to red in color as the numerical values between 0.000 and 0.100, and the molecule within ±18.51 Bohr3 range. The color red between the range 0.900 and 1.000 is the most probability to localized electrons appears on all the hydrogens in fluorophenyl-, 1-ethenamin-, pyrimidin-, piperazin-, pyrrolotriazin-, and methylpyrazol- groups, and core electrons in carbons and nitrogens in 1-ethenamin-, pyrimidin-, piperazin-, pyrrolotriazin-, and methylpyrazol- groups in the whole molecule shows strongly localized electrons. Moderated delocalized electrons show by green in color range between 0.450 and 550 appear in whole rings, and chain in the molecule. The blue in color between the range 0.000 and 0.200 denotes strong or the most probability to delocalized electrons in the molecule fluorophenyl-, pyrimidin-, piperazin-, pyrrolotriazin-, and methylpyrazol- ring groups, and lone-pair of electrons in nitrogen atoms in 1-ethenamin-, pyrimidin-, piperazin-, pyrrolotriazin-, and methylpyrazol- groups.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Localized orbital locator (LOL) study of Avapritinib
ce:section.ce:para: This study explains the localized and delocalized molecular orbitals locations for avapritinib [75–77]. The localized orbital locator (LOL) for avapritinib shown in Fig. 6 .

From the blue in color to red in color as the numerical values between 0.000 and 0.800, and the molecule within ±18.56 Bohr3 range. The blue color range between 0.000 and 0.160 denotes strong pi-delocalized orbital and red color range between 0.720 and 0.800 denotes weak pi-delocalized orbitals in avapritinib, in this molecule these orbitals appear adjacently, so delocalization of electrons occurs on nitrogens and carbons in fluorophenyl-, 1-ethenamin-, pyrimidin-, piperazin-, pyrrolotriazin-, and methylpyrazol- groups.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Molecular electrostatic potentials (MESP) of Avapritinib from electronic and nuclear charges
ce:section.ce:para: The electrostatic potential V(r) generated around a molecule by its nuclei and electrons which are treated as static charge distribution is a very useful property for studying and predicting molecular reactive actions. This is narrowly defined and can be calculated both experimentally and computationally [61,78,79]. The capacity has been especially useful as an indication of the positions or regions of the molecule to which the advancing electrophile is initially drawn, and has also been effectively extended to the analysis of associations requiring a certain optimal relative orientation of the reactants, such as between the product and its cellular receptor. Avapritinib molecule's MESP from electronic charges was generated using the data obtained in the previous calculation and is represented in Fig. S2.

From the blue in color to red in color as the numerical values between −0.1000 and 0.1000, and the molecule within ±17.71 Bohr3 range. The blue in color range from −1.000 to −0.040 appears on the nitrogen atoms rings and chain (electrophilic region) in 1-ethenamin-, pyrimidin-, piperazin-, pyrrolotriazin-, and methylpyrazol- groups, which are electron-rich sites, so electrophiles can easily attack these sites. The red in color range from 0.070 to 0.100 appears on all the protons in the amide group (nucleophilic region), fluorophenyl-, 1-ethenamin-, pyrimidin-, piperazin-, pyrrolotriazin-, and methylpyrazol- groups, which are electrons poor sites, so nucleophiles can easily attack these sites.

The electrostatic potentials from nuclear charges for avapritinib show in Fig. S3. From the blue in color to red in color as the numerical values between 0.000 and 50.000, and the molecule within ±17.79 Bohr3 range. The nuclear charge between 45 and 50, red in color shows the negative electrostatic potentials, which is strong attractions between protons and nuclei by core and lone-pair of electrons in heavy atoms like fluorine, nitrogens, and carbons in fluorophenyl-, 1-ethenamin-, pyrimidin-, piperazin-, pyrrolotriazin-, and methylpyrazol- groups, and these sites mostly undergo addition reactions and the nuclear charge between 17.500 and 22.500, blue in color shows the positive electrostatic potentials between protons and nuclei occurs in all the hydrogen atoms in fluorophenyl-, 1-ethenamin-, pyrimidin-, piperazin-, pyrrolotriazin-, and methylpyrazol- groups, which are mostly involved in substitution reactions.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Reduced density gradient (RDG) analysis of Avapritinib
ce:section.ce:para: The reduced density gradient is directly proportional to the electronic density of the molecule, which means a small reduced density gradient is low electronic density [80,81].

From the blue in color to red in color as the numerical values between 0.000 and 1.000, and the molecule within ±17.79 Bohr3 range. The red in color show the most probability to a high electronic reduced density between 0.9000 and 1.000 on the elements nitrogens in fluorophenyl-, 1-ethenamin-, pyrimidin-, piperazin-, pyrrolotriazin-, and methylpyrazol- groups. The moderately reduced density gradients show in color green is moderated reduced density is from 0.500 to 0.800 range bimolecular groups separated by sigma bond shown in Fig. S4.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Local information entropy (LIE) study of Avapritinib
ce:section.ce:para: This study explains the stability of the molecule. Entropy is a feature of probability distributions and can take to be a qualification of uncertainty. The high value of local information entropy is directionally proportional to the uncertainty of electrons in spatial distribution [82–85]. Fig. 7 shows local information entropy map for avapritinib.

From the blue in color to red in color as the numerical values between 0.000 and 1.000, and the molecule within ±17.79 Bohr3 range. All the elements except few atoms in avapritinib molecule having very low local information entropy value between 0.000 and 0.010, which means the molecule is more stable with low uncertainty electrons in the spatial distribution and blue in color depends upon masses of hydrogen, carbon, nitrogen, and fluorine atoms. Some elements in avapritinib molecule having moderated local information entropy value between 0.035 and 045, which means this molecule is stable with moderated uncertainty electrons in spatial distribution and bluish-green in color depends upon masses of carbons, and nitrogen in fluorophenyl-, and pyrrolotriazin- groups.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Non-covalent interactions (NCI) present in Avapritinib
ce:section.ce:para: A non-covalent interaction differs from a covalent bond by not involving the sharing of electrons, but involving more dispersed variations of electromagnetic interactions between molecules or within a molecule. The three-dimensional arrangement of large molecules, such as protein and nucleic acids, is important to non-covalent interactions. Additionally, they are also involved in many biological processes where large molecules bind to each other specifically but transiently. These interactions also have a strong impact on drug design, crystallinity and material design, self-assembly, and in the design of synthesis of tailor made organic molecules (Fig. 8 ).

A graph plotted energy against a reduced density gradient. The strong hydrogen bonds having the negative energy between the range −0.030 and −0.005 a.u. shows between fluorine and hydrogens in fluorophenyl-group and from amin- nitrogen in 1-ethanamin- to hydrohens in fluorophenyl- and pyrimidin- groups, the weak hydrogen bonds also called van der Waals force attraction energy between −0.005 and 0.005 a.u. from amin- nitrogen in 1-ethanamin- to hydrogens of methyl- groups in 1-ethanamine, and steric force also called strong repulsive force between the range 0.007 and 0.050 a.u. between fluorophenyl- and 1-ethenamin-, 1-ethenamin- and pyrimidin-, pyrimidin- and piperazin-, piperazin- and pyrrolotriazin-, and pyrrolotriazin- and methylpyrazol- groups.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Conclusions
ce:section.ce:para: The manuscript studies the structural features, energy features, energy descriptors, and other important physical properties of Avapritinib. UV–Visible spectra show an important peak at 273.3559 nm with 0.3834 oscillator strength. From the NLO property of avapritinib, dipole moment (μ) is 1.9787 times greater than urea and 7.1800 times greater than p-nitro acetanilide, hyperpolarizability (β) is 16.4314 times greater than urea and 1.7993 times greater than p-nitro acetanilide, mean polarizability (α0) is 15.3920 times greater than urea and 3.0444 times greater than p-nitro acetanilide, the anisotropy of the polarizability (Δα) is 15.8456 times greater than urea and 2.4350 times greater than p-nitro acetanilide and Molar Refractivity (MR) is 15.3949 times greater than urea and 3.0450 times greater than p-nitro acetanilide. The stability and intramolecular charge delocalization was studied using NBO. The reaction site properties like ALIE, ELF, LOL, MESP, MESP from nuclear charges, RDG, LIE, and NCI are explained in detail. And found that they happen mostly at mostly occur on fluorine, nitrogens, carbons, and protons in fluorophenyl-, 1-ethenamin-, pyrimidin-, piperazin-, pyrrolotriazin-, and methylpyrazol-groups.
